evidence: The statement asks whether h(N), the maximum size of a Sidon set in {1,...,N}, equals sqrt(N)+O(N^eps).
The problem asks for monochromatic solutions to the unit-fraction equation 1/a = 1/b + 1/c under any finite colouring of the integers.
Asking for a monochromatic solution in any finite colouring of the integers is a Ramsey-theoretic statement (Brown-Rodl, 'Monochromatic solutions to equations with unit fractions').
N(a,b) is the minimal length of an Egyptian-fraction representation a/b = sum 1/n_i; the docstring cites Erdos [Er50c] and Vose 'Egyptian fractions'.
N(a,b) is the minimal length of an Egyptian-fraction representation a/b = sum 1/n_i; the docstring cites Erdos [Er50c].
The cited result is Vose [Vo85], 'Egyptian fractions', bounding the minimal length N(b) of Egyptian-fraction representations.
The open question asks whether N(b), the maximal minimal Egyptian-fraction length over a/b, is O(log log b).
Asks whether every positive rational with squarefree denominator has an Egyptian-fraction representation a/b = 1/n_1 + ... + 1/n_k with special denominators.
Each denominator n_i is required to be the product of two distinct primes (omega(n_i)=2 and Omega(n_i)=2), i.e. a squarefree semiprime.
States every positive integer is an Egyptian fraction whose denominators are each products of three distinct primes.
Asks for two finite sets of primes P, Q with (sum_{p in P} 1/p)(sum_{q in Q} 1/q) = 1.
The equation is a product of two sums of unit fractions 1/p equalling 1; erdosproblems.com tags the problem 'unit fractions'.
The weakened version still asks for products of sums of unit fractions equal to 1, over pairwise-coprime sets excluding 1.
The barrier states any solution over sets of primes P, Q with Q nonempty uses at least 59 primes and prod P >= 2*10^56.
The theorem asks for a set B of density 0 (B.HasDensity 0) such that A+B contains all but finitely many integers.
The conclusion is about the sumset A+B (Pointwise) covering all sufficiently large integers; Lorentz [Lo54], 'On a problem of additive number theory'.
Asks whether any large multiset with sum of reciprocals 1/n exceeding K has a sub-collection of unit fractions summing into (1-exp(-cK), 1].
Asks for infinitely many pairs (m,P) of primes with sum_{p in P} 1/p = 1 - 1/m, an equation in unit fractions.
The file's reference is OEIS A054377 (primary pseudoperfect numbers), and solutions (m,P) are exactly witnessed by primary pseudoperfect m.
The variant conjectures that the set of primary pseudoperfect numbers (IsPrimaryPseudoperfect) is infinite.
Asks whether a finite set with sum of reciprocals 1/n below 2 always splits into two parts each with unit-fraction sum below 1 (disproved by Sandor).
Sandor's generalization: for any n>=2 a set with reciprocal sum below n admitting no n-part partition with each unit-fraction sum below 1.
